title: Did Gendo actually love Ritsuko
wikitext:
[]: Did Gendo actually love Ritsuko?             Quote Post by Squigsquasher » Wed Jun 04, 2014 8:46 am As we all know, Gendo was engaged in an affair with Ritsuko following Yui and Naoko's deaths. He also later sold her out to SEELE in Rei's place and then shot her during EOE. However, I think the case of whether or not he actually had feelings for her is up for debate. The most immediate piece of evidence is Gendo's follow up to "The truth is...". In EOE there is only silence (which even Ritsuko's VA found hard to work with)- however, in the manga, his lines are expicitly stated to be "Up until now, your actions were exemplary...I really did love you.". Whilst this alone initially seems like enough to prove that he did have affection for her, there are 2 problems: 1: Gendo is quite a proficient liar, and it is possible that he was not telling the truth (although what reason he would have for lying isn't quite clear). 2: This is exclusive to the manga, and therefore its relevance to NGE itself is questionable. So let's look a little deeper. (WARNING: Conjecture and wild mass guessing ahead.) One of the initial important points is that prior to entering a relationship with Ritsuko, Gendo was getting it on with Naoko. He called her and "old hag" behind her back and upon her death, he commented on her being a "Foolish woman" implying he never much cared for her. However, it's not certain this could be extended to Ritsuko; Naoko was shown to be a somewhat unpleasant individual, and it is possible that Gendo initially was attracted to her but was soon disillusioned with her, rather than simply using her without caring about her. Furthermore, if Gendo had found Naoko that unpleasant then he probably wouldn't have started a relationship with Ritsuko unless he had seen something in her. Another point of contention is that Ritsuko was sent in Rei's place as a "tribute" to SEELE. On the one hand, this could be because Gendo viewed her as "expendable" and did not care what happened to her. On the other hand, we do not see the full story regarding Ritsuko's interrogation; referring to the "sexual abuse" theory, it is possible that Ritsuko was one of SEELE's targets anyway and Gendo had no choice but to pick the lesser of two evils. It's also not clear if he knew what SEELE was going to do to her (assuming she was in fact raped), and thought they were just going to question her the way they did Misato and Fuyutsuki. It's also not entirely clear how "in the know" Ritsuko was regarding the whole affair; on the one hand, the shock she shows when told Gendo sent her instead of Rei could be that she did not know she had been offered up in Rei's place, or on the other hand she did know, but believed that Gendo knew what SEELE was going to do to her (once again assuming she was raped), whether or not this was the case. The whole problem is further compounded by the fact that Gendo is shown to hide his emotions very well; the only times he really shows his emotional side are when Rei is almost killed by Unit 00 going berserk, and when he realizes what a monster he had become during Instrumentality. One (albeit flimsy) piece of evidence that suggests he did in fact love Ritsuko comes from, ironically enough, Yui. During a conversation with Fuyutsuki, she comments that Gendo "is a really sweet man...it's just that nobody can see it...". This does suggest that Gendo is actually quite an affectionate, caring person, and shows he at least had the potential to love Ritsuko, although it doesn't provide much evidence that he actually did. Myself, I'm not quite sure. I for one am quite fond of the theory that Gendo is in many ways like an adult Shinji- specifically, I think he needs affection and someone to love him as an empotional crutch- however, he's much better at hiding it than Shinji. Personally I'm inclined to believe that he did love her to at least some degree, but really as far as I'm concerned the jury's still out. Anyway, does anyone have any thoughts regarding this quandry?

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Wed Jun 04, 2014 11:10 am If Yui had just died or if he'd been made aware that she willingly left him to be in the eva I think he might have gotten over her and been able to love Ritsuko. As it is though, I don't think he allows himself to see her as anything but a piece on his chessboard with benefits. He seemed to have a fondness for her but he isn't about to let such concerns get in the way of what his true goal.

[]:             Quote Post by Dataprime » Wed Jun 04, 2014 11:16 am This just popped into my head: Alternate Eva realty where Ritsuko was Shinji's mom!

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Wed Jun 04, 2014 11:16 am NemZ wrote: If Yui had just died or if he'd been made aware that she willingly left him to be in the eva I think he might have gotten over her and been able to love Ritsuko. As it is though, I don't think he allows himself to see her as anything but a piece on his chessboard with benefits. He seemed to have a fondness for her but he isn't about to let such concerns get in the way of what his true goal.

[]:             Quote Post by Ray » Wed Jun 04, 2014 3:16 pm I'm fairly sure their 'love' was purely biological.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Wed Jun 04, 2014 3:31 pm Gendo probably didn’t close his eyes when kissing Ritsuko either.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Wed Jun 04, 2014 4:22 pm While it's a gut feel, I don't think he truly loved Ritsuko. I'm unconvinced.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Wed Jun 04, 2014 4:45 pm I have to agree with NemZ and Bags. The potential was always there, the actuality never was. Naoko and Ritsuko are two more people that Yui pulled into her orbit and destroyed in her mad quest to be a grave marker.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Wed Jun 04, 2014 8:17 pm I'm usually inclined to defend Gendo but that's flat out impossible in this case. I'm pretty sure it was a 'lie back and think of England' thing for him, he was doing it purely to ensure her loyalty, and didn't even like her. I don't even see the potential, honestly. See how he talked of Naoko (and there's no reason to suppose it was any different with ritsuko; Their situations are always paralleled, rarely ever contrasted appart from Ritsuko being soewhat more repressed/ in denial of her lower urges.), "She's annoying!" They were chores, liabilities to him. Just contrast with how he handles Shinji or Rei, whom he sort of cares about but fails to act particularly productive towards due to complexes and inability - He'd never go and tell Shinji something he wants to hear to make him stay, even when him leaving would've been really unconvenient, and likes to have Rei in the same room with him even without a specific reason to prompt it ("Let' go eat") Note Ritsuko's words - She was previously aware tht she's probably imagining the love ("I'm needed, at least physically"), and bore it despite her growing unhappiness, but then realizes that she wasn't even his plaything or anything - it was purely for the plan, ie, Yui (ep 24: "dissapointed?! You never had any expectations to begin with! You didn't want anything from me, nothing at all...") There is a reason why the EoE line was left blank - because it's utterly irrelevant. That scene demonstrates the absurd power he has over her and maybe some fundamentally submissve natureshe could never shake of, maybe because she could never admit it as part of herself (as a prerequisite to dealin with it)because of the whole Naoko thing. Basically, even when she wants to blow him to hell, she can't do it without politely apologizing, she doesn't curse at him or anything. Whatever he says, it's totally weaponized, not something he's saying because it is some feeling of his, but to distract her so he can shoot her. And it works, she immediately starts crying and basically accepts her execution, even though her rational mind knows it bogus. That's basically the tragedy of her character, that she's got this cynical, rational mind capable of understanding, but stll remains a slave to her emotions; Living up to what we know is right isn't always easy, and sometimes we just can't bring ourselves to do it; And that's one of the tragedies of the human condition. Manga has no bearing on this because Gendo's character is quite different there - He's also shown to have persnally seduced Ritsuko, while the anime implies the Akagis both had the hots for him long before becoming his consorts and approached him , and that Ritsuko's infatuation was directly caused by some freudian complex thing about seeing him make out with Naoko. While the rape is unnecessarily pulled out of thin air and she's probably just naked for 'safety' (so she doesn't bring wapons or recording devices) or intimidation purposes, that he did send her as a cheap distraction ("The old men are busy with smething else.") is undeniable and even you can only explain it away with elaborate constructions of assumptions and contrievances. I also don't think that it's right to say that Gendo 'hides' his emotions - he's pretty blunt in most of what he says or does. It's probably more that he's naturally a detached type who can't into subtle social cues, and, in addition that, is better desribed as embittered and emotionally burned out, he can't bring himself to invest himsef more even with Rei he keeps a certain distance; He's someone who's thoroughly given up and doesn't care anymore. That may, at first, seem at odds with his huge plans, but actually, one thing flows from the other, instrumentality is a way of 'giving up' and perhaps of 'concentrating on what he does understand'/' can do' Yui's comment really speaks against anything because it really showcased how Ritsuko doesn't know shit about Gendo and has a totally wrong indea of him - She's looking for a dominant boss man to fill out for the missing male presence in her life/childhood, but Gendo's really a submissive, devoted type who'll take his wife's last name and blindly follow what she asks/plans she doesn't tell him all of, and never as much as questions her. That's what Gendo acts like when he likes you; Ritsuko's been mistaking disinterest for uncaring dominance ("Do with my body what you want, like you always do!"), allthough 'uncaring' is still not that unfitting an adjective. If Yui had never been a factor, he'd just ignore Ritsuko or screw her purely for the kicks. As for any Shinji parallels, one of the greatest differences between them is that while Shinji's outwardly emotional, Gendo's distantly calculating. He also has the ability to say, "Screw it, then let them hate me if they want to" and walk away, while Shinji always turns back and cares significantly more if he harms or wrongs other people. That's why he'd never do such manipulative crap, even more he wouldn't even have the ability if he had the intention so I don't think Ritsuko is an area where coparisions can be applied. But if Gendo had a 'crutch', it would still be Yui, that is, the hope to see her (and perhaps Shinji, too) again after instrumentality, which is why he completely throws himself into his work, it's literally all that keeps him going. Ritsuko doesn't have a clue who Gendo is; Gendo doesn't care about her beyond making her handle his test tubes.

[]:             Quote Post by Coldwire » Thu Jun 05, 2014 1:26 am Yeah, what Kendrix said. He was definitely using her just like Naoko. He might acknowledge her importance to Projet-E and respect her as a scientist but he could never love her, even if he wanted to. Ritsuko knew all along.

[]:             Quote Post by BC Baron » Thu Jun 05, 2014 2:11 am Kendrix wrote: I'm usually inclined to defend Gendo...

[]:             Quote Post by Ray » Thu Jun 05, 2014 3:19 am He loved Yui. . .He believed she was the only one who could ever love someone like him. And in a sick twisted way he was kind ve right!

[]:             Quote Post by Eleven » Thu Jun 05, 2014 6:19 am I mostly agree with Kendrix on this. I believe, the only woman (person?) Gendo ever loved was Yui. Maybe he liked Ritsuko to some extent, but I think every sign of affection he showed to her or her mother had the only purpose to manipulate them. Giving them little reasons to hope that someday he will return their feelings bound them to him. Maybe if he showed his disinterest openly, Naoko or at least Ritsuko would have been able to give up on him and walk away. But giving them sex and pretending there was at least the smallest chance of love made closure impossible for them. Anyway, I was never really convinced, that Rei I was telling the truth to Naoko. Might as well have made it up just to upset the "old hag". Little Reis face seems creepy and almost evil to me in that scene, so I always thought it was possible that Gendo never said such a thing. Gendo telling Ritsuko he loved her before her death: I always thought of that as a lie. Some twisted act of mercy. Maybe he thought "Might as well make her happy for the last few seconds of her life, doesn't matter anyway."

[]:             Quote Post by Rosenakahara » Thu Jun 05, 2014 9:27 am Simple answer:no, refer to other posts for longer answer

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Fri Jun 06, 2014 5:21 am Eleven wrote: Anyway, I was never really convinced, that Rei I was telling the truth to Naoko. Might as well have made it up just to upset the "old hag". Little Reis face seems creepy and almost evil to me in that scene, so I always thought it was possible that Gendo never said such a thing.

[]:             Quote Post by babaGAReeb » Fri Jun 06, 2014 9:57 am no ameego gendu is jus seducer who wuz usin her

[]:             Quote Post by Atropos » Fri Jun 06, 2014 2:12 pm Kendrix wrote: Ritsuko's comments also suggest that he wasn't particlarly gentle in bed, or receptive to her preferences, he probably just went and it got it over with.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Fri Jun 06, 2014 3:04 pm Ritsuko was firmly in control of an in denial of her emotional needs until the pressure became so great that she couldn't stand it anymore and the emotionless sex swung all the way around from fun to actively depressing.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Sat Jun 07, 2014 12:04 pm Atropos wrote: Rather, they suggest that a physical relationship used to be enough for her, but she wanted real emotional intimacy with him and he was unwilling to go that far. "I'm not happy anymore...not even when you make love to me" or something like that.

[]:             Quote Post by FinH » Tue Jun 17, 2014 7:48 pm Part of the reason Gendo was able to play the arch-manipulator and herd everyone about according to his designs was because he surrounded himself with people with phenomenally low self-esteem, whether because they were spawned in a big vat or because they were just profoundly fucked up individuals. I think this extended to personal/intimate relationships aswell. So, nah I don't think Gendo ever loved anyone at all (except possibly Yui, and even then it comes across to me as a twisted sort of obsession rather than healthy love - like he's enamoured with the concept of her rather than the actual woman who she was).
